title: STUSIL x POLARIS 24-Hour Challenge
description: 24-hour online challenge for high school students to solve community problems with innovation + tech. Pick a real problem, build a solution, win mentorship.
details: {"headline":"Build Real Solutions. Win Real Recognition.","subheadline":"24-hour online challenge for high school students to solve community problems with innovation + tech","challenge_desc":"STUSIL x POLARIS is a 24-hour online innovation challenge for high school students across Africa. Pick a real problem in your community → brainstorm → build a solution → submit in 24hrs. No coding experience needed. Just curiosity, teamwork, and the drive to make impact.","how_it_works_1":"Register - Sign up solo or with a team of 2-4 students","how_it_works_2":"Problem Drop - We release real community problem statements on June 14, 9am GMT","how_it_works_3":"Build for 24hrs - Ideate, design, prototype. Mentors available in Discord","how_it_works_4":"Submit & Get Judged - Submit your pitch deck/video. Winners announced June 17","open_to":"High school students aged 13-19 across Africa and world wide","format":"Individual or teams of 2-4","skills_needed":"None. We’ll provide templates, mentor support, and workshops before D-Day","winners_prize":"Certificate + Feature on STUSIL x POLARIS platforms + Mentorship session","participants_prize":"Official participation certificate + Access to innovation community","top_10_prize":"Special shoutout + Direct intro to youth innovation networks","timeline":[{"date":"June 1 - June 13","event":"Registration open + Pre-challenge workshops"},{"date":"June 14, 9am GMT","event":"Challenge starts - Problem statements released"},{"date":"June 15, 9am GMT","event":"Submissions close"},{"date":"June 17","event":"Winners announced"}],"what_you_build":"App mockup / Website prototype / Pitch deck / Video explanation / Social campaign. Focus is on problem-solving + clear thinking, not perfect code.","mentors_judges":"Learn from young founders, designers, and tech educators who’ve been where you are. Live Q&A + Discord support throughout the 24hrs.","faq_1_q":"Is it free?","faq_1_a":"Yes, 100% free to join","faq_2_q":"Do I need to code?","faq_2_a":"No. Ideas + prototypes are welcome","faq_3_q":"What if I’ve never done this before?","faq_3_a":"Perfect. We’ll teach you as you go","faq_4_q":"Can I join from any country?","faq_4_a":"Yes, fully online"}

title: Stusil Sprint: EdTech Hackathon
description: An intense 12-hour sprint focused on developing open-source tools to assist student collaboration and study group organization. Over 20 projects were successfully pitched.
details: {"headline":"Empowering Classroom Collaboration","subheadline":"A completed EdTech design challenge building peer-to-peer sharing applications","challenge_desc":"Our past EdTech Sprint gathered over 100 student builders to prototype next-generation homework help channels and notes repositories. Focus was placed on low-bandwidth setups for accessible learning.","how_it_works_1":"Register - Solo or team register","how_it_works_2":"Build - 12 hours from dawn to dusk","how_it_works_3":"Submit - Push project to Stusil and record a loom video","how_it_works_4":"Evaluate - Public votes and developer reviews","open_to":"All undergraduate and secondary students","format":"Solo or pairs","skills_needed":"HTML/CSS, Figma, or Loom explanation video skills","winners_prize":"Official feature and project stars promotion slot","participants_prize":"Participation certificate badge on portfolio","top_10_prize":"Invited to Stusil Incubation incubator private beta channels","timeline":[{"date":"April 15 - April 30","event":"Registration open"},{"date":"May 2, 8am GMT","event":"Hackathon Kickoff"},{"date":"May 2, 8pm GMT","event":"Workspace submissions close"},{"date":"May 5","event":"Results published"}],"what_you_build":"Figma mockup of an educational tool or simple responsive website dashboard.","mentors_judges":"Mentorship led by senior engineering candidates and ecosystem advisors.","faq_1_q":"Is it free?","faq_1_a":"Yes, always free","faq_2_q":"Do I need to code?","faq_2_a":"No, design decks were fully evaluated","faq_3_q":"What if I’ve never done this before?","faq_3_a":"Workshops are recorded for asynchronous viewing","faq_4_q":"Can I join from any country?","faq_4_a":"Yes, online challenge"}
